\medskip\noindent\textbf{Identity.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-value)}\quad $\lambda x.\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: v
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{The constant combinator $K$.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-value)}\quad $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: v
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Church numeral $2$.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-value)}\quad $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, x\, (x\, y)$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: v(v(vx))
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Successor.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-value)}\quad $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, y\, (x\, y\, z)$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: lambda vxx: vx(v(vx)(vxx))
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Addition.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-value)}\quad $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, \lambda w.\, x\, z\, (y\, z\, w)$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: lambda vxx: lambda vxxx: v(vxx)(vx(vxx)(vxxx))
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Multiplication.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-value)}\quad $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, x\, (y\, z)$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: lambda vxx: v(vx(vxx))
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Zero test.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-value)}\quad $\lambda x.\, x\, (\lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, \lambda w.\, w)\, (\lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, y)$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: v(lambda vx: lambda vxx: lambda vxxx: vxxx)(lambda vx: lambda vxx: vx)
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Self-application.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-need)}\quad $\lambda x.\, x\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
def _program():
    v_1 = CALL_BY_NEED_SENTINEL

    def v_0():
        nonlocal v_1
        if v_1 is CALL_BY_NEED_SENTINEL:

            def v_2(v_3):
                v_1_2 = CALL_BY_NEED_SENTINEL

                def v_0_2():
                    nonlocal v_1_2
                    if v_1_2 is CALL_BY_NEED_SENTINEL:
                        v_1_2 = v_3()(v_3)()
                    return v_1_2
                return v_0_2
            v_1 = v_2
        return v_1
    return v_0
program = _program()
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Local specialization in a cyclic term.}~\textnormal{(specialized to interpret)}\quad $Y\,(\mathtt{cons}\,(3 \times 3))$
The cyclic stream is fold-requiring, so the whole term stays interpreted; its closed element $3 \times 3$ is simply typable, hence a call-by-value island compiled to strict Python:
\begin{lstlisting}
(lambda v: lambda vx: lambda vxx: v(vx(vxx)))(lambda v: lambda vx: v(v(v(vx))))(lambda v: lambda vx: v(v(v(vx))))
\end{lstlisting}
The island runs and the interpreter folds around it, so the result is the same finite cyclic behaviour as pure interpretation, the stream $\mathtt{cons}\,9\,(\mathtt{cons}\,9\,\cdots)$ folded to a single cyclic node.

\medskip\noindent\textbf{The compiler, specialized: finding call-by-value islands.}\quad The compiler is untypable as a whole, since its fixpoint combinator $Z$ self-applies, so it stays interpreted; the specializer finds its 14 maximal closed simply-typable sub-terms, each a strongly-normalizing combinator compiled to a strict call-by-value island, while the recursive skeleton is left to the interpreter. The 9 with compact compiled forms are shown (the larger combinators are elided):
\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: v
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, x\, (x\, (x\, y))$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: v(v(v(vx)))
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, y\, (\lambda z.\, \lambda w.\, \lambda u.\, z\, (\lambda s.\, \lambda t.\, t\, (s\, w))\, (\lambda s.\, u)\, (\lambda s.\, s))\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: vx(lambda vxx: lambda vxxx: lambda vxxxx: vxx(lambda vxxxxx: lambda vxxxxxx: vxxxxxx(vxxxxx(vxxx)))(lambda vxxxxx: vxxxx)(lambda vxxxxx: vxxxxx))(v)
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, y\, (x\, y\, z)$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: lambda vxx: vx(v(vx)(vxx))
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, y$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: vx
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, x\, (\lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, y\, z)\, (\lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, z)$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: v(lambda vx: lambda vxx: vx(vxx))(lambda vx: lambda vxx: vxx)
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, x\, y$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: v(vx)
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, \lambda w.\, z\, x\, y$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v: lambda vx: lambda vxx: lambda vxxx: vxx(v)(vx)
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, x\, (\lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, y\, (y\, (y\, (y\, (y\, z)))))\, (\lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, z)$

lambda v: v(lambda vx: lambda vxx: vx(vx(vx(vx(vx(vxx))))))(lambda vx: lambda vxx: vxx)
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Self-hosting: the specialized interpret-with-islands form.}\quad A term with no whole-graph by-value certificate compiles to interpret-headed Python: it is reconstructed with the interpreter's node constructors and handed back to the interpreter, with its closed, shallow, simply-typable sub-terms spliced as compiled by-value islands. The compiler itself compiles this way; the full self-compiled compiler is committed as the generated module \texttt{\_generated\_compiler.py} (run by the interpreter on a quoted program, it emits the same Python as the in-process compiler). Here is a small term in the same form, $\mathtt{factorial}\,(2\times 3)$, whose multiplication sub-term is spliced as a by-value island:
\begin{lstlisting}
_k0 = make_var(1)
_k1 = make_var(0)
_k2 = make_app(_k1, _k1)
_k3 = make_app(_k0, _k2)
_k4 = make_lam(_k3)
_k5 = make_app(_k4, _k4)
_k6 = make_lam(_k5)
_k7 = value_island(lambda v: v(lambda vx: lambda vxx: lambda vxxx: vxxx)(lambda vx: lambda vxx: vx))
_k8 = make_app(_k7, _k1)
_k9 = value_island(lambda v: lambda vx: v(vx))
_k10 = make_app(_k8, _k9)
_k11 = value_island(lambda v: lambda vx: lambda vxx: v(vx(vxx)))
_k12 = make_app(_k11, _k1)
_k13 = make_var(2)
_k14 = make_var(3)
_k15 = make_app(_k0, _k14)
_k16 = make_app(_k1, _k15)
_k17 = make_lam(_k16)
_k18 = make_lam(_k17)
_k19 = make_app(_k13, _k18)
_k20 = make_lam(_k0)
_k21 = make_app(_k19, _k20)
_k22 = value_island(lambda v: v)
_k23 = make_app(_k21, _k22)
_k24 = make_lam(_k23)
_k25 = make_lam(_k24)
_k26 = make_lam(_k25)
_k27 = make_app(_k26, _k1)
_k28 = make_app(_k0, _k27)
_k29 = make_app(_k12, _k28)
_k30 = make_app(_k10, _k29)
_k31 = make_lam(_k30)
_k32 = make_lam(_k31)
_k33 = make_app(_k6, _k32)
_k34 = (lambda v: lambda vx: lambda vxx: v(vx(vxx)))(lambda v: lambda vx: v(v(vx)))(lambda v: lambda vx: v(v(v(vx))))
_k35 = value_island(_k34)
_k36 = make_app(_k33, _k35)
_k37 = interpret(_k36)
compiled_compiler = _k37
\end{lstlisting}
